name: Todoist REST API
what_it_does: Todoist is a task management platform with a REST API v2 and a lower-level Sync API. Agents can create, read, update, and close tasks, manage projects and sections, assign labels, set due dates, and receive webhook events on task state changes.
use_cases: Capturing agent-generated tasks into a human's task inbox
Marking tasks complete after automated workflow execution
Creating project structures and populating them with tasks programmatically
Monitoring task completion to trigger downstream agent actions
Syncing tasks from external systems (email, Slack, calendar) into Todoist
not_for: Complex project management with dependencies and Gantt charts
Team collaboration at scale (better tools exist for that)
Storing large structured data — tasks have limited metadata
best_when: An agent needs to create or manage actionable tasks for a human, particularly in a GTD-style personal productivity workflow.
avoid_when: You need rich project planning features like task dependencies, resource management, or timeline views.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 recommended for multi-user apps; personal API token for single-user agent use. OAuth scopes exist but are coarse (data:read, data:read_write, data:delete). API tokens available from settings for direct agent use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5 active projects, 5 collaborators per project, no reminders, no labels
pricing.notes: Free tier is functional for basic agent task creation. Pro unlocks reminders, filters, and labels which are useful for agent-created tasks. API access is available on all tiers.

performance.rate_limits: 450 requests per 15 minutes per user token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors return structured JSON with error type and message. HTTP status codes are used correctly. Some edge cases (like invalid recurrence strings) return 400 with limited detail on what specifically is invalid.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: REST API v2 has no idempotency key support. The Sync API uses a client_id pattern for deduplication. Agents using the REST API should check for duplicate tasks before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API v2 and Sync API have overlapping but different capabilities — choose one and be consistent
No idempotency keys on REST API; duplicate task creation is possible on network retry
Due dates use a natural language parser that can misinterpret dates in edge cases
Webhooks require HTTPS endpoint with valid certificate — local dev requires tunneling
Task IDs are string-encoded integers; do not treat as numeric
Completed tasks are not returned by default in task list endpoints — require explicit filter
Rate limit is per user token, not per app — shared tokens across agents will exhaust limits quickly

score_rationale: Todoist has a clean, well-documented REST API with OAuth, webhooks, official SDKs, and reasonable rate limits. The main gaps for agents are no idempotency keys on the REST API and coarse OAuth scopes. The Sync API offers more power but more complexity. Overall one of the better task management APIs for agent integration, especially for personal productivity automation.

security_score_components.security_notes: API token per user. OAuth2 for apps. SOC2 Type II. Task data may contain sensitive personal information. Webhook HMAC validation. No granular API scopes.
